name: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency
subtitle: Structural Steel | Construction Engineering — EN 1993-1-1:2022 §7.2, AISC 360-22 Appendix 2

inScope: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Second Moment of Area About Strong Axis (I_x)

standards.name: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency
standards.description: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency — Structural Steel, Construction Engineering

formulas.expression: Cd_map = {SS: 5/384, FF: 1/384, FP: 1/185, cant: 1/8}; Cd = Cd_map[end_condition]
formulas.reference: Beam deflection coefficients — Roark's Formulas for Stress & Strain 8th Ed. §8.1; EN 1993-1-1 §7

formulas.expression: E_steel = 210000; I_m4 = Ix_cm4 * 1e-8; L_m = span_m; delta_DL_mm = Cd * w_dead_kn_m * 1000 * Math.pow(L_m, 4) / (E_steel * 1e6 * I_m4) * 1000
formulas.reference: δ = C_δ × w × L⁴ / (E × I); E = 210,000 MPa for steel — EN 1993-1-1 §3.2.6

formulas.expression: delta_LL_mm = Cd * w_live_kn_m * 1000 * Math.pow(span_m, 4) / (210000e6 * Ix_cm4 * 1e-8) * 1000 + (P_point_kn > 0 ? 1/48 * P_point_kn * 1000 * Math.pow(span_m, 3) / (210000e6 * Ix_cm4 * 1e-8) * 1000 : 0)
formulas.reference: Live load + point load deflections superimposed; midspan point load δ = PL³/(48EI) for SS beam

formulas.expression: delta_total_mm = delta_DL_mm + delta_LL_mm; delta_allow_mm = span_m * 1000 / deflection_limit_ratio
formulas.reference: Allowable = L/n; total = δ_DL + δ_LL — EN 1993-1-1 §7.2 Table NA.3 / AISC DG3
L/d efficiency: floors 18–24; purlins 20–30; crane beams 12–16 — SCI P355 §3.2 design guidance
Camber = 75% of dead load deflection per AISC Code of Standard Practice §6.1; δ_LL / δ_allow ≤ 1.0

validation.message: Live load deflection exceeds allowable limit — increase section size (I_x), reduce span with intermediate support, or accept lower deflection limit
Total deflection exceeds L/100: extreme deflection — verify input values; beam is severely undersized or span is unrealistic for this load
L/d > 30 with significant live load: shallow beam will deflect excessively — consider L/d ≤ 20 as starting point for preliminary sizing under office / industrial loads

warnings.message: Camber specified on non-simply-supported beam: verify that end connection allows free rotation and that camber direction is consistent with actual dead load deflection shape
Deflection utilisation > 85%: marginal — consider one section deeper; increasing depth by 50 mm typically reduces δ by 15–20% for the same weight
Dead load deflection > L/400: significant long-term creep and ponding risk on flat roofs — EC3 recommends δ_DL + creep < L/300 for roofs per §7.2.1
Precision deflection limit on shallow beam: floor vibration may govern before deflection limit — check natural frequency per SCI P354 (fn > 4 Hz for office floors)
Long cantilever (> 3 m): deflection tip grows as L³; very sensitive to load position — verify back-span anchorage and connection rotation capacity; dynamic check mandatory per SCI P076

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required

legalNotice: ⚠ Engineering decision support only — verify before use. This is a technical simulation, not professional engineering advice.
